chapter: Chapter 8: A SHIRT IN THE MARKET

questions.question: Explain the journey of a shirt from cotton to the market.
questions.answer: A shirt starts with cotton farming, then goes through ginning, spinning, weaving, tailoring, and finally reaches the retailer before being sold to customers.

questions.question: Describe the role of farmers in the production of a shirt.
questions.answer: Farmers grow cotton, harvest it, and sell it to ginners or traders. They are responsible for producing the raw material for the shirt.

questions.question: Explain how ginning, spinning, and weaving contribute to shirt production.
questions.answer: Ginning cleans the cotton, spinning turns it into yarn, and weaving converts the yarn into cloth, which is the main material for making shirts.

questions.question: Discuss the role of tailors in making a shirt.
questions.answer: Tailors cut and stitch the cloth into a shirt, ensuring proper design, size, and finishing. They turn raw cloth into the final wearable product.

questions.question: Explain the importance of traders and retailers in the market.
questions.answer: Traders and retailers distribute shirts to markets and shops, making them accessible to customers. They determine prices and profit margins.

questions.question: Describe how prices of a shirt increase from farm to market.
questions.answer: The price increases at each stage due to costs of labor, materials, transportation, and profit for farmers, workers, traders, and retailers.

questions.question: Explain how workers’ efforts and skills affect shirt production.
questions.answer: Workers contribute through planting, harvesting, processing cotton, weaving, tailoring, and selling. Skilled work ensures quality shirts and timely production.

questions.question: Discuss challenges faced by farmers and workers in the shirt supply chain.
questions.answer: Challenges include low wages, market fluctuations, poor working conditions, and high labor intensity, which affect income and quality of life.

questions.question: Explain the concept of fair trade and ethical practices in shirt production.
questions.answer: Fair trade ensures that producers and workers receive fair wages and work in safe conditions, while promoting sustainable and ethical production methods.

questions.question: Describe how market demand influences shirt production and pricing.
questions.answer: Higher demand increases production and may raise prices, while lower demand may reduce production and profits for producers and sellers.

questions.question: Explain the difference between small-scale and large-scale production.
questions.answer: Small-scale production involves manual labor and local markets, while large-scale production uses machines, factories, and mass distribution to wider markets.

questions.question: Discuss the role of transportation in the shirt supply chain.
questions.answer: Transportation moves cotton, cloth, and finished shirts between farmers, factories, traders, and markets, ensuring timely delivery and availability.

questions.question: Explain how technology has changed shirt production.
questions.answer: Technology has improved efficiency through mechanized spinning, automated looms, and modern tailoring machines, reducing labor and increasing production speed.

questions.question: Describe the connection between local markets and global trade in clothing.
questions.answer: Local shirt production can link to global trade through exports. Traders and factories may sell shirts internationally, impacting prices and labor demand.

questions.question: Discuss the environmental impact of shirt production.
questions.answer: Shirt production affects water usage, chemical pollution from dyes, waste management, and cotton farming practices, influencing sustainability.

questions.question: Explain how supply and demand affect the livelihood of farmers and workers.
questions.answer: Higher demand can increase income, while low demand may reduce wages. Market fluctuations directly affect the livelihood of those involved in production.

questions.question: Describe the importance of quality control in shirt production.
questions.answer: Quality control ensures that shirts meet customer expectations, reduces wastage, and maintains the reputation of producers and retailers.

questions.question: Explain the role of cooperatives and associations in supporting farmers and workers.
questions.answer: Cooperatives provide access to markets, fair prices, shared resources, and support in production, helping farmers and workers improve income and security.

questions.question: Discuss how pricing strategies affect customers and sellers.
questions.answer: High prices may reduce customer purchases, while low prices may decrease seller profits. Proper pricing balances affordability with fair income for producers.

questions.question: Explain the challenges of maintaining sustainability in shirt production.
questions.answer: Challenges include high water usage, chemical pollution, worker exploitation, and ensuring fair wages while remaining profitable.

questions.question: Describe how consumer awareness can influence the shirt market.
questions.answer: Consumers who value fair trade, quality, and sustainability encourage producers and retailers to adopt ethical practices and improve working conditions.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between local labor and industrial production.
questions.answer: Local labor provides the workforce for spinning, weaving, and tailoring. Industrial production relies on skilled labor to maintain efficiency and quality.

questions.question: Discuss the economic and social benefits of fair wages for workers.
questions.answer: Fair wages improve living standards, education, health, and reduce exploitation, leading to more stable communities and better production quality.

questions.question: Explain the role of government regulations in the shirt market.
questions.answer: Government regulations set standards for labor, environment, quality, and trade, ensuring safety, fairness, and sustainability in production.

questions.question: Why is it important to study the production and marketing of a shirt in understanding society?
questions.answer: Studying shirt production shows how people, labor, trade, prices, and sustainability are interconnected, helping students understand economic, social, and ethical issues in society.
